phases.label: Level 1
phases.why: At level 1 your chain-clone burst escapes beat the wall-frost poke control in a straight exchange. Play the front foot — make Anivia answer you, not the other way around.

phases.label: Level 2
phases.why: Your chain-clone burst escapes out-muscle Anivia's wall-frost poke control at level 2 — this window is yours, so force the trades and tax every step toward the wave.

phases.label: Level 3
phases.why: At level 3 your chain-clone burst escapes beat the wall-frost poke control in a straight exchange. Play the front foot — make Anivia answer you, not the other way around.

phases.label: Levels 4-5
phases.why: Your chain-clone burst escapes out-muscle Anivia's wall-frost poke control through levels 4-5 — this window is yours, so force the trades and tax every step toward the wave.

phases.label: Level 6
phases.why: At the level-6 breakpoint your chain-clone burst escapes beat the wall-frost poke control in a straight exchange. Play the front foot — make Anivia answer you, not the other way around.

phases.label: First item
phases.why: Slight edge on first item: your chain-clone burst escapes beat Anivia's wall-frost poke control on the margins. Take the short trades you start — just don't force a coin-flip all-in off a small lead.

phases.label: 2+ items
phases.why: Respect window: at two-plus items the wall-frost poke control beat your chain-clone burst escapes cleanly. Trade only off Anivia's cooldowns and keep the wave where the lead can't cash out.

phases.label: Level 1
phases.why: Q-auto the trades — the sigil mark is half your damage; never throw Q without the detonation plan.

phases.label: Level 2
phases.why: W online: the Q-W double-proc chunk is live, and the return trip makes it free. The lane flinches now.

phases.label: Level 3
phases.why: E joins: Q-E chains hold targets for the full string. Pick one tether window per wave.

phases.label: Levels 4-5
phases.why: W-poke the wave contacts and return before his answer — distortion math is the whole champion.

phases.label: Level 6
phases.why: Mimic online — the double-dash crosses the lane and the burst doubles. Most mids die to the first full chain.

phases.label: First item
phases.why: First item spike: Q-W-RW deletes squishies. Spend the lane lead before scaling lanes wake.

phases.label: 2+ items
phases.why: Aurelion Sol's stardust ramp control own this window — your chain-clone burst escapes don't answer them at two-plus items yet. Farm what's safe and let the next spike re-price the lane.

phases.label: Level 1
phases.why: Q-auto the trades — the sigil mark is half your damage; never throw Q without the detonation plan.

phases.label: Level 2
phases.why: W online: the Q-W double-proc chunk is live, and the return trip makes it free. The lane flinches now.

phases.label: Level 3
phases.why: E joins: Q-E chains hold targets for the full string. Pick one tether window per wave.

phases.label: Levels 4-5
phases.why: W-poke the wave contacts and return before his answer — distortion math is the whole champion.

phases.label: Level 6
phases.why: Mimic online — the double-dash crosses the lane and the burst doubles. Most mids die to the first full chain.

phases.label: First item
phases.why: First item spike: Q-W-RW deletes squishies. Spend the lane lead before scaling lanes wake.

phases.label: 2+ items
phases.why: Late LeBlanc picks: W the flank, chain the carry, mimic the exit — and vanish before the obituary prints.

phases.label: First item
phases.why: On first item the numbers lean your way — chain-clone burst escapes edge out the point-click spear bullying when both sides play it straight. Press the small advantages: wave position, first poke, cooldown ticks.

phases.label: 2+ items
phases.why: Late LeBlanc picks: W the flank, chain the carry, mimic the exit — and vanish before the obituary prints.

phases.label: Level 1
phases.why: Slight edge at level 1: your chain-clone burst escapes beat Yasuo's windwall dash-weaving on the margins. Take the short trades you start — just don't force a coin-flip all-in off a small lead.

phases.label: Level 2
phases.why: At level 2 the numbers lean your way — chain-clone burst escapes edge out the windwall dash-weaving when both sides play it straight. Press the small advantages: wave position, first poke, cooldown ticks.

phases.label: Level 3
phases.why: Your chain-clone burst escapes out-muscle Yasuo's windwall dash-weaving at level 3 — this window is yours, so force the trades and tax every step toward the wave.

phases.label: Levels 4-5
phases.why: Through levels 4-5 your chain-clone burst escapes beat the windwall dash-weaving in a straight exchange. Play the front foot — make Yasuo answer you, not the other way around.

phases.label: Level 6
phases.why: Slight edge at the level-6 breakpoint: your chain-clone burst escapes beat Yasuo's windwall dash-weaving on the margins. Take the short trades you start — just don't force a coin-flip all-in off a small lead.

phases.label: First item
phases.why: On first item the numbers lean his way — the windwall dash-weaving out-tick your chain-clone burst escapes in even exchanges. Don't bleed for pride; take the trades his cooldowns hand you.

phases.label: 2+ items
phases.why: Late LeBlanc picks: W the flank, chain the carry, mimic the exit — and vanish before the obituary prints.

phases.label: Level 1
phases.why: Slight edge at level 1: your chain-clone burst escapes beat Yone's double-blade spirit dives on the margins. Take the short trades you start — just don't force a coin-flip all-in off a small lead.

phases.label: Level 2
phases.why: At level 2 the numbers lean your way — chain-clone burst escapes edge out the double-blade spirit dives when both sides play it straight. Press the small advantages: wave position, first poke, cooldown ticks.

phases.label: Level 3
phases.why: Your chain-clone burst escapes out-muscle Yone's double-blade spirit dives at level 3 — this window is yours, so force the trades and tax every step toward the wave.

phases.label: Levels 4-5
phases.why: Through levels 4-5 your chain-clone burst escapes beat the double-blade spirit dives in a straight exchange. Play the front foot — make Yone answer you, not the other way around.

phases.label: Level 6
phases.why: Mimic online — the double-dash crosses the lane and the burst doubles. Most mids die to the first full chain.

phases.label: First item
phases.why: On first item the numbers lean his way — the double-blade spirit dives out-tick your chain-clone burst escapes in even exchanges. Don't bleed for pride; take the trades his cooldowns hand you.

phases.label: Level 1
phases.why: Q-auto the trades — the sigil mark is half your damage; never throw Q without the detonation plan.

phases.label: Level 2
phases.why: W online: the Q-W double-proc chunk is live, and the return trip makes it free. The lane flinches now.

phases.label: Level 3
phases.why: E joins: Q-E chains hold targets for the full string. Pick one tether window per wave.

phases.label: Levels 4-5
phases.why: W-poke the wave contacts and return before his answer — distortion math is the whole champion.

phases.label: Level 6
phases.why: Mimic online — the double-dash crosses the lane and the burst doubles. Most mids die to the first full chain.

phases.label: First item
phases.why: First item spike: Q-W-RW deletes squishies. Spend the lane lead before scaling lanes wake.

phases.label: 2+ items
phases.why: Late LeBlanc picks: W the flank, chain the carry, mimic the exit — and vanish before the obituary prints.
